plugins.name: superfix
plugins.description: Intelligent dev orchestrator — classifies GitHub issues or freeform descriptions, assembles the right skill set, and drives work to a merged PR

plugins.name: supersocials
plugins.description: End-to-end social media skill — strategy, copywriting, video scripts, content calendars, visual briefs, and analytics across Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, X, YouTube, Facebook, Pinterest, Threads, and Bluesky

plugins.name: expert-panel
plugins.description: Adversarial expert panel review — profiles proposals, routes to skill or agent workflow, runs 4 independent experts across adversarial axes, and produces a decision dossier with must-fix, should-fix, unresolved objections, cross-panel tensions, and a steel-manned alternative.

plugins.name: company-intel
plugins.description: Deep background research and competitive intelligence on any company or organisation — entity verification, financial intelligence, people profiling, digital footprint, and competitive analysis.

plugins.name: topic-intel
plugins.description: Deep, multi-source intelligence research on any non-company topic — markets, industries, policies, regulations, academic fields, technology categories, geographic communities, trends, and public figures.

plugins.name: visual-review
plugins.description: Visual QA skill — renders PDFs and HTML at 300 DPI, runs Quick Pass or Full Review, produces action-first HOLD/FIX IF TIME/NOTE reports. Covers clipping, overlaps, font fallback, density imbalance, typography, brand drift, 8 mandatory bottom-edge checks, Paged.js structural patterns, form-aware checks, type-specific checks for slides/books/reports, commercial press-readiness checklist (BleedBox/TrimBox, ICC OutputIntent, TAC, spot colours), and conditional stack assessment via Phase 1.5.

plugins.name: visual-stack-review
plugins.description: Document production stack analyser — detects renderer from PDF metadata, package.json, and HTML signals; classifies document type; recommends stay-and-fix or migration with 5-component effort breakdowns. Deep-dive references for 11 tools (Paged.js, WeasyPrint, Prince, Typst, headless Chrome, Playwright/Puppeteer, LaTeX, Pandoc, React-PDF/ReportLab, InDesign/Affinity, wkhtmltopdf). Quarterly matrix review procedure included. Expert-panel reviewed: print production claims corrected, migration costs re-rated, CVE carve-out from stay+fix.
